cards.name: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting
cards.address: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting Co.
cards.blurb: Grab a morning coffee and claim a seat outside while Los Gatos slowly wakes up around you.
cards.why: Cool morning air and good coffee is a perfect combo before you get moving.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water, shaded and easy, great for stretching your legs and clearing your head.
cards.why: The trail is peaceful on weekday mornings and the creek views are genuinely pretty this time of year.

cards.name: Savannah-Chanelle Vineyards
cards.address: 23600 Congress Springs Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Solid local pick for a meal.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum has real mummies, actual ancient artifacts, and a surprisingly deep collection for a San Jose afternoon.
cards.why: It's one of those places that sounds random but ends up being genuinely fascinating.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Picchetti Winery for a glass on the hillside patio, surrounded by old vines and rolling views.
cards.why: The hilltop setting makes a weekday afternoon wine stop feel like a real escape.

cards.name: Yvette Spa by Jackie
cards.address: 248 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Yvette Spa by Jackie on Main Street in Los Gatos, a solid way to close out the day.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly where you want to end up.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs on this easy, shaded trail along the creek — great pace for a cool morning, whether you walk fast or just meander.
cards.why: Cool temps and no rain make this the perfect low-key start to the day.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art plus a sculpture garden you can wander at your own pace. Give the main galleries a good look and then spend time outside with the sculptures.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a Saturday morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Get the brisket plate and do not skip the mac and cheese. This is the move for lunch when you want something serious.
cards.why: Best BBQ in the South Bay, full stop.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Flat and scenic along the Bay, good for shaking off that second glass of wine with a casual walk or bike ride before the evening.
cards.why: Gets you moving before dinner without requiring any real effort.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab the hummus — it comes warm with olive oil and fresh pita and it is genuinely worth the trip. Fast, casual, and filling before the show.
cards.why: Quick, delicious, and done in time to get to the evening event.

cards.name: Poems and Dances from the East
cards.address: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
cards.blurb: Hear world premiere classical works blending East and West with Chinese instruments.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a well-pulled espresso or pour-over from one of San Jose's best roasters. Grab a seat and ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything else better.

cards.name: Curator-led tours: The Woven Pixel and Motherboards
cards.address: San Jose Museum of Quilts & Textiles, 520 S. 1st St, San José, CA 95113
cards.blurb: Tour The Woven Pixel at the Quilts Museum, then head to the Art Museum for a Motherboards exhibition tour.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: San Jose Museum of Quilts & Textiles

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden, which is absolutely worth a lap or two in early May when things are in bloom.
cards.why: It's free, it's beautiful, and it's one of the better-kept secrets in San Jose.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs along this smooth, scenic trail that winds beside the creek through Campbell — great for a casual stroll or light jog in the cool afternoon air.
cards.why: It's a relaxed way to shake off rose garden wandering before you head toward Los Gatos for dinner.

cards.name: Stevenson Baseball Field
cards.address: 1698 Montecito Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Catch a casual recreational baseball game or simply hang out at this well-kept community field — a low-key spot to wind down before your evening reservation.
cards.why: It keeps you moving without overdoing it on a cool May evening with dinner still ahead.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drive down to Dio Deka in Los Gatos for dinner. Get a table on the patio if the evening holds up, and don't skip the mezze to start.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner worth dressing up a little for.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Walk the terraced Japanese gardens, cross the wooden bridges, and take your time on the upper paths where the fog sometimes still hangs over the hillside in the morning. Bring a light jacket.
cards.why: Cool and quiet on a Thursday morning — you'll basically have the place to yourselves.

cards.name: Ukulele Orchestra of Great Britain
cards.address: 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Watch the Ukulele Orchestra of Great Britain perform their eccentric string arrangements.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: On Writing Beloved Characters and Powerful Portrayals of Resilience with Jason Reynolds
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Hear author Jason Reynolds discuss writing compelling characters and stories of resilience.
cards.why: These folks are genuinely hilarious and tight as a band — it's a rare show.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Nice spot to get some air.
cards.why: A beloved family spot — lake views, easy trails, kids love the little train

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail at a easy pace — the path is shaded, flat, and long enough that you can go as far as you feel like before turning back.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the ideal window for a walk before the day fills up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art plus a sculpture garden you can wander on your own schedule — take your time with the outdoor pieces before heading inside for the galleries.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most slept-on art spaces in the whole Bay Area.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Get the brisket plate and don't skip the mac and cheese — this is the kind of BBQ you eat slowly and without regret.
cards.why: Best BBQ in the South Bay, full stop.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real Egyptian mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and planetarium-style exhibits tucked inside a San Jose neighborhood — budget a full two hours because there's more here than you'd expect.
cards.why: This museum punches way above its weight and most locals have never been.

cards.name: Music on Castro: Innuendo
cards.address: Downtown Mountain View, 200 Block of Castro Street
cards.blurb: Hear singers and songwriters perform free at Downtown Mountain View.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a hike through Fremont Older Open Space — the foothill trails open up to sweeping views of the valley and it stays uncrowded even on weekends. Layers help since it'll be cool until midday.
cards.why: Cool morning air and zero crowds make this the ideal way to shake off the week.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Main Street Cupertino for lunch — the stretch has a solid lineup of Asian restaurants and casual spots, so pick whatever sounds good and grab a table on the outdoor plaza.
cards.why: It's the most easygoing midday stop in Cupertino and you're already in the neighborhood.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up Montebello Road to Ridge Vineyards for a tasting — the mountain setting is legitimately stunning and the wines are serious without being stuffy. Grab a glass and sit outside if it's not too breezy.
cards.why: A cool, partly cloudy Sunday is honestly perfect for a hilltop tasting with big valley views.

cards.name: Poetry Open Mic at the Cupertino Library - Poetry Month Celebration
cards.blurb: Share or listen to multilingual poetry from writers and poets of all ages in a welcoming open mic setting.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at San Pedro Square Market in downtown San Jose — load up from a few different vendors, grab a patio seat if the evening warms up a little, and take your time with it.
cards.why: A dozen vendors under one roof means everyone at the table gets exactly what they want for dinner.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail and shake off the morning fog. It's flat and easy, so just pick a direction and go until you feel like turning around.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window before the day fills up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the galleries and spend some real time in the sculpture garden out back. Contemporary work, no admission charge, and never crowded on a weekday.
cards.why: Free art on a Tuesday feels like a little cheat code.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in with a glass or two and take your time with the pour list. Ask what's pouring well today and go from there.
cards.why: Los Gatos wine on a Tuesday afternoon is honestly the move.

cards.name: Svetlana’s Massage
cards.address: 15066 Los Gatos Almaden Rd Ste 120-A, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll through the open-air shops and cafes of Main Street Cupertino, picking up anything from local goods to a mid-afternoon coffee to recharge before dinner.
cards.why: A relaxed browse through a walkable shopping district is a nice way to ease into the evening without overcommitting.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: This is a proper sit-down Greek dinner, so dress up a little and grab a patio table if the evening cooperates. The food is rich and the atmosphere delivers.
cards.why: It's the nicest table in Los Gatos and a great way to go into book club well-fed.

cards.name: Sci-Fi & Fantasy Book Club
cards.blurb: Discuss Light From Uncommon Stars by Ryka Aoki with fellow sci-fi and fantasy readers.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's a easy, unhurried way to ease into the morning right in Palo Alto before your watercolor class.

cards.name: ArtSEE Expression: Crystallized Color - Watercolor Magic
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Make watercolor art on salt-and-glue canvases where science meets creative expression.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Order the hummus — it comes warm, topped with olive oil and spices, and eat it with fresh pita. Simple, fast, and seriously good.
cards.why: You won't find better hummus on the Peninsula, full stop.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Give yourself the full two hours here. The history of computing is way more dramatic than it sounds, and the exhibits are dense in the best way.
cards.why: This museum genuinely surprises people who aren't even into tech.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a glass on the outdoor patio at Picchetti and wind down with the hillside views. It's a relaxed, no-fuss tasting experience.
cards.why: A late-afternoon pour on a 60-degree day with a view is hard to beat.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Stroll through the Rodin sculpture garden on the Stanford campus as the evening cools down. It's free, open-air, and genuinely beautiful at dusk.
cards.why: Few places in the Bay Area are this peaceful and this free at the end of a Sunday.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail through the trees, take your time, and let the morning cool air do its thing before the day gets going.
cards.why: 53 degrees and peaceful is a great combo for a morning walk.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through interactive exhibits tracing the full arc of computing history, from punch cards to personal computers — plenty to explore at your own pace over a couple of hours.
cards.why: It's a great way to keep the morning momentum going indoors before your afternoon wine stop.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a pour on the patio at Picchetti, one of the oldest wineries in the South Bay, and take in the hillside views with a glass in hand.
cards.why: Midday wine on a cool Thursday with no crowds is a genuinely good time.

cards.name: Vidovich Vineyards
cards.address: 18101 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stop in at this intimate hillside winery for a tasting of their estate-grown varietals with sweeping views of the Cupertino foothills.
cards.why: It bridges the gap between Picchetti and your Los Altos evening without adding much backtracking.

cards.name: Petals & Prosecco
cards.blurb: Celebrate community with neighbors over drinks while supporting local arts and connection.
cards.why: It's a mellow, social warm-up that leads perfectly into the drawing session right after.

cards.name: Patterned Flower Drawing for Adults and Teens
cards.blurb: Draw intricate patterned flowers using simple line techniques inspired by traditional art styles.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Los Gatos Creek Trail — flat, shaded path along the creek, perfect for a easy walk to shake off the morning.
cards.why: Cool temps and low rain chance make this a solid morning move.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art and wander the sculpture garden out back. Free admission, genuinely good contemporary work, and never crowded on a weekday.
cards.why: It's one of the most slept-on art spots in the South Bay and it's free.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Picchetti Winery sits on a historic homestead in the Cupertino hills. Grab a glass on the patio and take in the view — peacocks roam the grounds and the setting is genuinely charming.
cards.why: A lazy Thursday afternoon with a glass of wine on a hillside patio is hard to beat.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll through Ridge Vineyards' hilltop estate and taste their celebrated Montebello wines while soaking in sweeping views of the Santa Cruz Mountains.
cards.why: It's a natural extension of your afternoon at Picchetti and keeps the wine-country mood going before your evening in Los Altos.

cards.name: Petals & Prosecco
cards.blurb: Celebrate community with neighbors over drinks while supporting local arts and connection.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Scotty McCreery
cards.address: 14831 Pierce Road
cards.blurb: Hear country music from Scotty McCreery in an outdoor vineyard setting.
cards.why: It's a low-key but genuinely fun way to cap the day with live music in a beautiful outdoor setting close by.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's right in Saratoga, so you can ease into the day close to home before the afternoon vineyard visit.

cards.name: Saratoga Rotary Art Show
cards.blurb: View artwork from local artists displayed in this rotating exhibition at West Valley College.
cards.why: It's a low-key arts browse that fills the slot perfectly and keeps you in Saratoga ahead of your lunch stop next door.

cards.name: Savannah-Chanelle Vineyards
cards.address: 23600 Congress Springs Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Quick stop to grab a glass and take in the vineyard views before your afternoon commitment locks in.
cards.why: Savannah-Chanelle is a beautiful pre-lunch pit stop and it's right in Saratoga wine country.

cards.name: Kintsukuroi Movie Screening
cards.blurb: Watch a film exploring the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery and what it teaches us about healing.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Red Light Day Spa
cards.address: 16579 Los Gatos Almaden Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Red light therapy, a quick massage, or just a facial to decompress and shift gears before dinner.
cards.why: A spa hour between wine and dinner makes the whole evening feel intentional.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Vasona Lake at dusk is genuinely pretty — walk the lake loop, watch the light fade over the water, and let the day wind down naturally.
cards.why: Los Gatos on a Saturday evening has good energy and this park is the perfect cool-down walk.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the day with a locally roasted pour-over or espresso drink — this is one of the best coffee stops in San Jose, full stop.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything else better.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: You practice English speaking and listening while learning about other cultures in conversation.
cards.why: It's a lively community event and the kind of thing that makes the South Bay feel like home.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Sit inside or out depending on how the weather feels.
cards.why: Easiest lunch call in downtown San Jose.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden — spring is exactly when this place earns its reputation, with blooms coming in all over the grounds.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and worth it in April.

cards.name: Dia del Niño (Children's Day) Community Celebration
cards.address: 1680 Foley Avenue, San Jose, CA
cards.blurb: Let families play games, get face painting, eat pizza, and celebrate children together.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ — get the brisket plate and don't skip the mac and cheese. It's the kind of BBQ that holds up every single time.
cards.why: Best BBQ in the South Bay, and you'll have earned it by this point in the day.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a proper cup from one of San Jose's best roasters. Locally roasted beans, consistently excellent espresso, and a good spot to ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee before a busy morning is non-negotiable.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Take a quick walk along the creek-side trail, enjoying the tree cover and fresh morning air before the birthday festivities kick off.
cards.why: A peaceful buffer between coffee and the event that gets you moving without straying far.

cards.name: Discovery Duck Name Reveal Birthday Bash
cards.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
cards.blurb: Help reveal the museum's rooftop duck's name, try hands-on activities, and celebrate their 35th birthday.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara has free world-class contemporary art and a sculpture garden worth wandering. No admission, no rush, just good work on the walls and a peaceful outdoor space.
cards.why: Free art on a cool afternoon is a pretty easy yes.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the outdoor shopping district, browse local boutiques, and grab a coffee to recharge between museum and dinner.
cards.why: It's a low-key way to stretch your legs and window-shop before heading down to Los Gatos.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dio Deka is the nicest dinner in Los Gatos. Greek-inspired menu, a beautiful patio, and a vibe that earns dressing up a little. Order the mezze, share something from the grill, and take your time.
cards.why: After a full day, you deserve a proper dinner at a place that feels like an occasion.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 344 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at ZombieRunner, a quirky little coffee shop on California Ave. Grab a coffee and settle in before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a local institution and a great way to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up to the Stanford foothills and hike the Dish loop. The views over the Bay stretch for miles and the trail has just enough climb to feel satisfying.
cards.why: Cool morning air and clear skies make this the best window to be on the hill.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum and spend a couple hours genuinely losing track of time. The exhibits go deeper than you'd expect and the whole place is surprisingly gripping.
cards.why: It's one of the best museums in the region and you don't have to be a tech person to love it.

cards.name: Vidovich Vineyards
cards.address: 18101 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drive up Montebello Road to Vidovich Vineyards for an afternoon pour on the hillside. It's a small family operation with big views and really good wine.
cards.why: A Thursday afternoon at a hilltop winery with no crowds is hard to beat.

cards.name: Patterned Flower Drawing for Adults and Teens
cards.blurb: Draw intricate patterned flowers using simple line techniques inspired by traditional art styles.
cards.why: A free evening art class is a genuinely fun way to close out a Thursday.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the day with a locally roasted pour-over or espresso — this is the kind of coffee shop that actually cares about what's in your cup.
cards.why: Good coffee makes the whole day better, and this place does it right.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: You practice English speaking and listening while learning about other cultures in conversation.
cards.why: A handmade card hits different, and this makes it easy to actually do it.

cards.name: Back A Yard Caribbean Grill
cards.address: Back A Yard, San Jose
cards.blurb: Get the jerk chicken plate — it's the move here, full stop. One of the most reliably great lunches in downtown San Jose.
cards.why: You'll be glad you didn't skip lunch on a day this packed.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours inside with the real mummies, ancient artifacts, and unexpectedly great exhibits that make this place one of San Jose's best-kept secrets.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fascinating museum that most locals still haven't been to.

cards.name: Mother's Day Craft: Cardmaking
cards.address: 290 International Circle San Jose
cards.blurb: Design and create a handmade card celebrating your mother or mother figure with your own personal touch.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit a few different vendors and build your own dinner — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a drink and find a spot at the communal tables.
cards.why: It's the easiest way to get a great dinner without committing to one thing.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a locally roasted pour-over or espresso drink. Chromatic takes their coffee seriously and it shows in every cup.
cards.why: Good coffee before anything else is just the right call.

cards.name: Japanese Paper Arts Workshop
cards.blurb: Master the 1500-year-old Mizuhiki knot technique using Japanese decorative paper cords.
cards.why: Free, hands-on, and genuinely interesting — a great way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton punches way above its weight for a free museum. Walk through the contemporary galleries and spend some time in the sculpture garden out back — it's the kind of place that takes you by surprise.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a grey afternoon is a genuinely good use of a few hours.

cards.name: Wee Love Art: Bernice Bing
cards.address: 290 International Circle San Jose
cards.blurb: Learn about artist Bernice Bing and create art inspired by her style and impact.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ means brisket that holds up to any comparison, plus mac and cheese that you'll think about later. Order a plate, get a side of everything, and don't rush it.
cards.why: After an afternoon of art, low-key BBQ is exactly the right gear shift.

cards.name: Green Healthy SPA
cards.address: Branham Ln, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a massage at Green Healthy Spa. Book a session and let someone else handle the tension you didn't know you were carrying.
cards.why: Ending a full day with bodywork is a treat that actually holds up the next morning too.

cards.name: GK Pastry & Sourdough
cards.address: 251 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GK Pastry on Main Street — grab a coffee and something from the pastry case and ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a genuinely great local spot and the perfect low-key Monday morning move.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Take one of the easier loops through Rancho San Antonio and breathe some fresh air before the day fills up. The trails are well-marked and the scenery is worth it even on a cool morning.
cards.why: Cool temps and low crowds on a Monday make this the best version of that hike.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Computer History Museum for a couple hours. The exhibits are genuinely fascinating whether or not you work in tech, and there's always something unexpected to find.
cards.why: It's one of those places that sounds nerdy but ends up being one of the best afternoon stops in the South Bay.

cards.name: Poetry Open Mic
cards.blurb: You share or listen to poetry in a welcoming open-mic setting with fellow enthusiasts.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a locally roasted pour-over or espresso drink. Chromatic takes their coffee seriously, and it shows in every cup.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Saturday morning before the day picks up.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market and build your own lunch by mixing vendors. Tacos from one spot, a drink from another, then grab a seat on the patio if the afternoon warms up.
cards.why: No better downtown San Jose lunch spot when you want options without committing to one menu.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, real artifacts, and more interesting than you'd expect from a Saturday afternoon in San Jose.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the weirdest and coolest museums in the Bay Area and most people have never been.

cards.name: Sky Head Spa
cards.address: 1403 W San Carlos St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Squeeze in a quick head and scalp massage at Sky Head Spa before your evening event. It's a short, focused treatment that actually leaves you feeling reset.
cards.why: Good way to decompress before the evening kicks off, and they close at 6 so the timing works.

cards.name: Play Your Way - Sensory Spring
cards.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
cards.blurb: Explore hands-on museum activities designed for autistic children in a calm environment.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose

cards.name: Back A Yard Caribbean Grill
cards.address: Back A Yard, San Jose
cards.blurb: Dig into bold Caribbean plates like jerk chicken and rice and peas at this laid-back San Jose spot.
cards.why: A flavorful, filling dinner fits perfectly between the sensory spring event and wrapping up the evening.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a hike through Fremont Older Open Space — the foothill trails are uncrowded on weekday mornings and the views from the ridgeline are genuinely worth the climb.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a trail before the day gets going.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch from one of the Asian restaurants in the mix — the area has great options for a weekday sit-down.
cards.why: It's right in the heart of Cupertino and an easy landing after the hike.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Picchetti Winery and spend the afternoon with a glass on the outdoor terrace. The old ranch setting is beautiful and the pours are generous for a Tuesday afternoon.
cards.why: A winery afternoon on a weekday feels like a small luxury you actually earned.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside paths, watch the ducks, and unwind on the grassy lawns before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: A peaceful outdoor buffer between the winery and dinner that keeps you in the Los Gatos area right on time.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka is a proper sit-down Greek meal in Los Gatos. Go for the patio if the evening cooperates, and don't skip the mezze to start.
cards.why: It's the right kind of dinner before a 7 PM event — not too rushed, actually delicious.

cards.name: Tuesday Travel Nights: Having a Devil of a Time in Tasmania
cards.blurb: You hear Ann Resch's stories of exploring Tasmania through her family's decades-long connection.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: The Whodunit? Mystery Book Club At Los Altos Library
cards.blurb: Discuss this month's mystery novel with fellow readers in a monthly book club at 10:15 am.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Take an easy walk along Los Gatos Creek Trail, one of the flattest and most pleasant stretches of green in the South Bay. The creek path is shaded and quiet on weekday afternoons.
cards.why: Cool temps today make it ideal for a walk without breaking a sweat.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Oren's Hummus on University Ave for lunch. Get the hummus masabacha and a pita, maybe a side of cauliflower. It's fast, filling, and genuinely one of the better lunches you can have for under $20.
cards.why: You won't find better hummus anywhere on this side of the Bay.

cards.name: Music on Castro: The Purely Collective
cards.address: Downtown Mountain View, 200 Block of Castro Street
cards.blurb: Enjoy free live music from The Purely Collective downtown.
cards.why: Free live music on a Wednesday evening is hard to beat.

cards.name: Stanford Cardinal Baseball vs. USF Dons Baseball
cards.address: 151 Sam McDonald Mall
cards.blurb: Watch Stanford baseball battle USF in a college matchup.
cards.why: It's a great way to cap the evening with some live sports energy right after the Castro Street concert.

cards.name: Bill's Cafe
cards.address: Bill's Cafe, Willow Glen
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Willow Glen institution. Get the eggs benedict or a breakfast burrito and take your time settling in.
cards.why: Best breakfast in the South Bay and a great way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering through one of the most genuinely surprising museums in California. Real mummies, real hieroglyphics, and a peaceful garden courtyard — right here in San Jose.
cards.why: It's an easy, fascinating way to spend a cool morning without being outdoors.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Practice speaking English with peers, pick up conversational skills, and learn about different cultures.
cards.why: A genuinely fun low-key activity that doesn't require any craft experience.

cards.name: DIY Beaded Jewelry: Crafts for Adults and Seniors
cards.address: 1243 San Tomas Aquino Rd. San Jose
cards.blurb: Create beaded jewelry and bookmarks while socializing with other adults and seniors in the community.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka in Los Gatos. Dress up a little, sit on the patio if it's not too cold, and work through the Greek menu properly.
cards.why: It's the nicest dinner in Los Gatos and a great way to close out the night.
